cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which answer is correct based on the passage. The question is asking what can be inferred from the passage. The passage talks about the new George Washington Presidential 1 coin. It mentions that the Presidential 1 coins are the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. The question gives four options. Looking at the options: A) There are no words on the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. The passage says that the new coins have an inscription on the edge, which is the first time since the 1930s. But does that mean the Sacagawea Golden Dollar doesn't have any inscriptions? The passage doesn't explicitly say that. It just says that the new coins have inscriptions on the edge for the first time since the 1930s. So maybe Sacagawea coins don't have edge inscriptions, but the passage doesn't say there are no words at all on them. So A might be incorrect because we can't confirm it from the text. B) The new coin can buy more than the old coin. The passage says the coins are the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar, but it doesn't mention their purchasing power. The value is $1 in both cases, so their buying power depends on other factors not discussed here. So B is probably not correct. C) The new coin takes on a white color. The passage says the new coins are gold-colored, same as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. So C is incorrect.
